h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: Which detail proves that before I rewrite the caption?
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: the sentence is not pressure just because it sits in the complaint file; the file has to show it being aimed at someone.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was heading toward identifying a single proof detail that makes the sentence act as pressure, expecting a concrete evidential item.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: In the tray, “attached” only keeps papers moving together. At the claims desk, the same slip changes status if it says “for response,” names an office, or requires a reply. So let the mark decide its own verb.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to L1's conflation of placement with pressure, the tutor abandons direct evidence-listing and invents a role/phrase turn—moving from static desk to dynamic role-based object work with the attachment slip, letting the learner inhabit the document's changing function.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: In the tray, “attached” only keeps papers moving together. At the claims desk, the same slip changes status if it says “for response,” names an office, or requires a reply. So let the mark decide its own verb.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor immediately seizes the learner's newly articulated framing of the attachment mark as still nagging (L3) and recasts the object-work task, shifting from static desk placement to role-based circulation versus action, precisely adapting the investigation to the learner's revised question.
